name: WheeledLab (UW Robot Learning)
role: Source of the car asset (MuSHR/HOUND) + dynamics/domain-randomization recipe used to train the frozen low-level locomotion controller. Provides the rigid-body 'body' the demo cars use.
decision: Use WheeledLab's car USD asset + dynamics/domain-randomization recipe to train the single-agent low-level controller (see lowlevel-controller). Single-agent is exactly right here — the controller only needs locomotion, not coordination. Do NOT expect a ready multi-car policy or a pre-baked setpoint tracker; verify whether any pretrained weights are downloadable, else train the controller from the shipped configs.
outputs: reference tasks: drifting, elevation, visual nav
facts: Platforms: MuSHR, HOUND (~1/10 RC cars); F1Tenth coming soon.
Backend: Isaac Lab on Isaac Sim (pins Isaac Sim v4.5.0 + IsaacLab v2.0.2).
Single-agent (verified in env source: one robot per env). Fine for our use — it only supplies locomotion.
Trains DIRECTLY in full car physics (why its policies transfer zero-shot to real RC cars) -> good template for our low-level controller.
uncertainties: pretrained checkpoint download links unconfirmed; shipped policies are task-specific, not a generic setpoint tracker -> plan to train the controller yourself
risks: weights may not be published -> train the low-level controller from configs (single-agent, modest)
Isaac/PhysX-native -> the low-level controller must run in Isaac (matches the chosen execution engine)
effort: hours to reuse the asset; folds into the lowlevel-controller training run
